module_mood_contract: permission-giving opener — student reads: 'even the person who built this hit all these walls; the LLM is genuinely non-deterministic; I am ready to run my own loop'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy doc (M6 in detail §Opener): 'Goes first because permission works before the work, not after.' Maintainer block line 79: 'In-room opener for M6. Lands before the exercise.' This is a ceiling-demo / permission-giving opener, not a pattern-naming meta-frame. Rule 1 permits ceiling-demo openers explicitly.
M6 is practitioner-fluency / encoding mood, not a contrast-mood module. Contrast landed at M4→M5. Rule 2 targets contrast-mood openers. Not in scope here.

Maintainer block line 81: 'Delivery: In-room opener for M6. Lands before the exercise; permission-giving, not credibility-performance.' Strategy doc M6 Opener section: 'Permission-giving, not credibility-performance.' Mood is explicitly named and honoured. Trace phase_1 mood_score = 9; above the 8+ floor.

The unease (non-determinism, universal struggle) is framed as a technical condition the student will encounter — not shame for past errors. L37–45: 'Everyone struggles. Surprises happen. The LLM is not a deterministic machine.' Dispassionate third-person condition-naming. No one is blamed.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: L55–65 ('What the loop is for') leads with the discipline: 'Your job is not to stop the drift. Your job is to catch it and encode what you learned.' The failures named earlier are evidence for the discipline, not the discipline itself. L44–45: 'A rule in context is not a rule in the output. Taste closes the gap.' Positive discipline as the close of the generalisation section.

Strategy doc M6 Opener: 'Plain stats from the generation session — turns, nudges, banned-word slips, reframes reversed — then the concrete things that went sideways, then the generalisation, then the landing that hands off to the student's own loop.' File matches this spec exactly: numbers section (L12–14), 'Things that went sideways' (L16–33), 'The generalisation' (L35–45), 'Then I continued...' (L47–53), 'What the loop is for' (L55–65). No downstream teaching moment preempted.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: This is a lecture with no fenced prompt blocks. Forcing functions are an exercise concern. Not applicable.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No authored conversation cues, pair-shares, pause beats, or 'discuss with...' sentences appear anywhere in the body. The memo is addressed to the student reading alone. The 'Your turn.' closer at L65 is a hand-off to the exercise, not a cohort-management instruction.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — three observable Monday-morning outcomes.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Rule 45 applies to exercises, not lectures. This file is a lecture/opener.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch named for every forcing function.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No forcing functions in a lecture file. N/A.
No fenced prompt blocks in this lecture file.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
Mood lands: 8+/10 at every teaching beat.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Single phase (whole body). Trace mood_score = 9. Teaching beat classification: this opener IS a teaching beat (its job is to land the permission-giving frame and the condition of non-determinism — these are the pedagogical moves). Mood floor 8+ applies. 9 > 8. Close mood_score = 9.

The opener lands permission-giving (not the full practitioner-fluency mood that closes M6). L65: 'Your turn.' — hands off to the exercise, does not claim the student now has practitioner fluency. The practitioner-fluency close lives at module end (after exercises + Debrief), not here. Strategy arc intact.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands across persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Teaching moment: 'everyone struggles; surprises happen; the LLM is not a deterministic machine.' (L37–42). The generalisation is isolated in its own section with three standalone sentences — structurally unreachable to skip without skipping the entire section header. A fast-operator persona who skims reads the numbers + the three-sentence generalisation and lands the frame. A deep reader gets the full anecdote texture. Trace: persona re-reads the generalisation once. No persona variation allows missing it. L47–49 ('Then I continued to make the compounding...') adds the second beat — even the author ran the loop and encoded — which lands the full chain (struggle → encode) for mid-competence readers.

M6 Big Idea is 'Spot gaps, build the loop.' This opener does not carry the Big Idea itself (it is permission-giving before the work, per strategy). However it does not violate Big Idea fidelity — it names the condition that makes the loop necessary (non-determinism, drift, universal struggle) and hands off explicitly to the student's own loop run at L57–65. Strategy explicitly assigns Big Idea delivery to the exercises, not this opener. The opener honours its designated role.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc: file's close hands off to M6 exercise open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: L55–65 ('What the loop is for') names the next hour explicitly: 'The next hour you will diagnose gaps in the M5 packaged run. You will author a skill...' This directly cues the M6 exercise sequence (spot-gaps-build-the-loop.md). L65 'Your turn.' closes the lecture and opens the exercise. Handoff is clean.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing function lives in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture file — no fenced prompts. N/A.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lecture placement: meta-frame lectures are closers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: See check_lectures.md rule 1 verdict above. This is a permission-giving ceiling-demo opener, not a meta-frame closer. Strategy validates the placement explicitly.
